threat_model: An Mfidel conformance receipt can become unsafe if it treats normalized Unicode, root-letter modeling, consonant/vowel splitting, or lossy transliteration as equivalent symbolic identity. It can also become an authority bypass if external SDK/kernel bindings are marked validated without local digest and exact-preservation evidence. The mitigation is a schema-backed receipt that denies normalization/decomposition operations, validates local Python digests and exact sequence witnesses, and keeps TypeScript/Rust bindings in AwaitingEvidence with explicit gap refs.

required_checks.check: live runtime import authority and terminal closure remain denied
external TypeScript/Rust SDK and kernel bindings remain AwaitingEvidence
redaction check confirms raw secret material remains excluded
policy bypass check confirms Unicode normalization, Unicode decomposition, root-letter modeling, consonant/vowel splitting, and overlay structural decomposition remain denied
receipt integrity check confirms local implementation digests and summary counts cannot drift silently
audit visibility check confirms proof coverage matrix includes the conformance receipt surface

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode receipt validates local Python substrate evidence and keeps TypeScript/Rust SDK/kernel bindings AwaitingEvidence until digest, no-normalization, and exact-preservation refs exist.

mitigations: deny Unicode normalization, decomposition, and recomposition
deny root-letter modeling and consonant/vowel splitting
verify exact fidel sequence preservation through runtime lookup
keep external SDK/kernel bindings AwaitingEvidence until witnessed
